title: Definition of a domestic student in New Zealand - Ministry of Education
content:
Te tautuhinga o te tauira taiwhenua ki Aotearoa

## Definition of a domestic student in New Zealand
We clarify the definition of domestic student for schools and further education institutions.
- Definition for schools
- Definition of domestic student (tertiary education)
Section 10 of the Education and Training Act 2020 defines a domestic student as an individual who is:
- a New Zealand citizen, or
- the holder of a residence class visa granted under the Immigration Act 2009 who satisfies the criteria (if any) prescribed by regulations made under subsection (2), or
- a person of a class or description of persons required by the Minister, by notice in the Gazette, to be treated as if they are not international students.

## Definition for schools #
The classes of persons enrolled with schools who are required to be treated as if they are not international students are detailed in the education notice below.
Education (Domestic Student: Primary and Secondary Education) Notice 2026 – New Zealand Legislation
To enquire about eligibility to enrol in New Zealand schools as a domestic student, contact us.
Phone: +64 4 463 8000
Email: enquiries.national@education.govt.nz

## Definition of domestic student (tertiary education) #
The classes of persons enrolled with a tertiary institution or registered establishment who are required to be treated as if they are not international students are detailed in the education notice below.
Education (Domestic Student: Tertiary Education and Vocational Education and Training) Notice 2025 – New Zealand Legislation
- Regulations to better support international students
- Education circular 2020/08
